Asset Name Type Status Workspace Directory Notes description Game Design Document (GDD) Documentation Completed /docs/docs/game/ Core loop, mechanics definitions, and status synergy systems. palette Pixel Art Spritesheets Visual Planned /static/img/sprites/ 32x32 sprite designs for player and enemy entities. music_note Dungeon Soundtracks Audio Planned /static/audio/bgm/ Ambient loop files for stage exploration and combat states. settings Bullet Wave Configs Configuration Planned /src/config/waves.json JSON mapping active wave projectile patterns. schema Engine Core Loop Schema Diagram Completed /docs/docs/engine/ Flowchart detailing engine lifecycle loops. layers UI Mockups Design Planned /static/img/ui/ Interface layouts for combat options and menus.
Thank you for your interest in contributing to Char2D! Whether you are writing engine code, designing mechanics, or improving our documentation, your help is highly appreciated.
To ensure a smooth collaboration, please follow these guidelines when contributing to the project.

Naming Conventions: Use snake_case for Type, Method, Property, and Variable names. Safety First: Make proper use of Beef's memory safety features. Minimize usage of unsafe blocks unless absolutely necessary for performance. Documentation: Always document public APIs and document complex logic blocks.

Pixel Art: Align all visual assets to a 64x64 pixel grid. Export files in .png format with transparent backgrounds. Audio & Soundtracks: Background music and sound effects must be compressed as .ogg files. Ensure loop metadata tags are included for BGM. Asset Registry: When adding new asset files, make sure to update the Asset Registry document.

Branch Naming: Prefix branches with their type, for example: Feature/Your-Feature-Name Bugfix/Issue-Description Docs/Update-Contribution-Guidelines Commit Messages: Keep commit messages concise, clear, and descriptive (e.g., Feat(Engine): Implement event system parser). PR Submissions: Open a Pull Request targeting the main branch. Provide a summary of the changes and reference any related open issues and workflows with screenshots or videos.
Here is how you can set up and run the different components of the Char2D workspace.

The documentation site is built using Docusaurus. Follow these steps to run it locally: Navigate to the docs directory: cd docs Install dependencies: npm install Start the development server: npm start Access the site: Open your browser and navigate to http://localhost:3000/Char2D/. Any changes made to the Markdown files will hot-reload automatically.

To run or build the Char2D game engine/demo: Ensure you have the Beef IDE installed on your machine. Open the Beef IDE and load the workspace file (Workspace.beefproj or folder) from the repository root. Select your target configuration (e.g., Debug or Release) and press F5 to compile and run the engine.

documents.t: Architecture breakdown of the Char2D game engine built in Beef Lang.
The following diagram maps the primary systems, core components, and their sub-modules:
Design logs, mechanics descriptions, and gameplay systems.

Phase Location / Context Key Mechanics & Activities location_city 1. Pre-Dungeon Big City Draft a team of up to 3 members using captured monsters and hired NPCs. Purchase items/gear, upgrade traits, accept sidequests, and talk to townsfolk to prepare for the run. layers 2. Dungeon Procedural Map Traverse map nodes representing a floor (5 floors total) to locate the portal and advance, managing paths and node choices dynamically. swords 3. Combat Battle Field Pick skills, actions, items, or defenses via a bottom command panel, and then control a soul hitbox inside a grid space to active dodge waves of enemy attacks. history 4. Post-Dungeon Run Resolution Wipe all standard run-specific currency and items. Save a Memory or Blessing item providing persistent starting perks, traits, quests or exceptions for subsequent runs.

Node Type Description flash_on Normal Combat Standard battle against a regular group of regional monsters. whatshot Super Combat Elite battle against tougher, randomized enemy compositions. redeem Treasure Node Find loot, items, or modifiers that can shift run synergy. storefront Utility Node Visit the shopkeeper or rest/heal at the Tavern. military_tech Boss Node Defeat the floor boss to unlock progression to the next floor. explore Event Node Text-based events or choices with variable outcomes like weather for example.

Combat Stage Objective Layout & Gameplay Mechanics ads_click 1. Strategy Selection Input party commands Party stands on the left, enemies on the right. Commands are selected via the bottom command panel. videogame_asset 2. Dodging Phase Survive bullet-hell waves Once inputs are submitted, control a soul hitbox inside a grid space to dodge active projectile patterns. Closely avoiding an attack generates TP, while being hit reduces HP of the targeted units.

Command Usage & Costs Mechanics & Effects sports_kabaddi Fight Weapon & Monster Skills (Consumes TP) Triggers interactive minigames where damage scales based on timing/performance. Utilizes Damage Types that affects damage based on weakness and resistances (Slash, Pierce, Blunt) and status synergies. shield Defend Evade, Guard, or Counter (Generates TP) The evade shrinks soul hitbox to slip through tight patterns, the guard gives a flat percentage reduction of incoming damage and the counter deals flat return damage when hit by projectiles. healing Items Utility Consumables (No TP Cost) Consumes inventory items to heal HP/TP or apply temporary stats/synergy buffs or activate special events. auto_awesome Action Tactical Contextual Skills Context-dependent actions to inspect enemy stats, weaken their staggering threshold, or attempt to capture monsters.

Synergy Damage/Effect Type Mechanics & Decay Behavior water_drop Bleed Potency Damage Deals damage whenever the target performs any action. Decays 1 count per action. local_fire_department Burn Potency Damage Deals damage at the end of the target's turn. Decays 1 count per turn. bolt Charge Damage Multiplier Self-stacking damage multiplier. Fully consumed on the next skill execution. trending_down Sinking Mercy Meter Boost Drains 1 count on hit to increase the target's Mercy Meter by the current potency. adjust Poise Crit Chance Stat Increases crit chance by potency x 5%. Crits consume 1 count and deal +20% damage. heart_broken Rupture Hit Bonus Damage Inflicts extra damage on hit equal to the status potency. Decays 1 count per hit. vibration Tremor Stagger Gauge Drain Drains count on hit to reduce the target's Stagger threshold by its potency.

Meter Visual Indicator Baseline Behavior & Maximum Effects favorite Health Red Bar Represents vital status. Reaching 0 defeats/kills the combatant. dangerous Stagger Yellow Shield Bar Shielding boundary. Reaching 0 stuns the target for 1 round; resets next turn. handshake Mercy Blue Harmony Bar Behavioral alignment. Higher values reduce enemy attack power and allow sparing/capturing.

This repository contains the documentation, technical specs, design assets, and development logs for the Char2D game engine and the game powered by it. Use the sidebar or choose a section below to get started:

settings Game Engine Technical breakdown of engine architecture: rendering pipeline, event loops, physics, and input polling. sports_esports Game & Design Game design docs, dungeon phase structures, enemy types, and turn-based combat mechanics. folder_open Artifacts & Assets File registry tracking spritesheets, sounds, animations, configurations, and mapping assets. groups How to Contribute & Run Rules for code standards, git workflow, asset integration guidelines, and running the project locally.

The project codebase is hosted on GitHub. Follow the instructions there for setting up the local runtime environment and integrating custom assets. If you could also star the repo, that would be of immense help for us!
